{"key_event":"Boom-era state building and labor repression","reasoning":"The export-credit boom, Panama indemnity, Kemmerer reforms, and infrastructure expansion substantially increased fiscal, monetary, and territorial capacity, though foreign enclave dominance and local limits kept the state short of fully strong. Civil society gained from unions, socialist organization, labor congresses, worker press, and improved communications, but Conservative hegemony, the Ley Heroica, strike repression, and the Banana Massacre sharply constrained those gains.","society_change":0.4,"society_power":5.5,"state_change":0.7,"state_power":6.7}
